scores.notes: Good idiomatic ops charm code with proper event.workload, add_layer/replan, config access, and HTTP health check. Minor issues: ops.pebble.Layer() constructor usage is non-standard (typically a plain dict is passed to add_layer, not a Layer object instantiated this way — though it may work); the gold standard uses dict literals directly. The on-check-failure key 'health' matches the check name. The _database_ready check uses relation.data.get(relation.app) which is slightly off — relation.data is a RelationData object not a plain dict, so .get() may not work as expected (should be relation.app in relation.data or try/except). Port config access adds a minor assumption not in the question. Overall well-structured but these API nuances could cause runtime issues.
